en.value:
Education: The focus of the education program is to reach the most marginalized communities. High priority is given to achieve universal primary education with a focus on gender parity and ensuring access to quality learning opportunities to all with a focus on girls and marginalized groups.

	Child Protection: All vulnerable and survivor children will benefit from advocacy and capacity building of law implementing agencies program conducted at the national level. While the focused program to be implemented in the Mid and Far Western regions will particularly reach to the following children:

	
		30,000 vulnerable children including orphans, Badi, and socially and economically poor;
	
		5,000 survivors of violence;
	
		6,000 conflict affected children; and 5,000 women vulnerable to Chhaupadi. 


	Child rights: The primary beneficiaries of the project are children up to age of 18 years. SCNN ensures that no children are discriminated however; SCNN will focus its target to children who are from most marginalized and disadvantaged population, including promoting girls participation. The secondary target group of this component of the project are:

	 

	
		The Ministry of Women, Children and Social Welfare and the focal points on child rights implementation
	
		The networks for child rights and participation
	
		The child clubs and their networks
	
		The National Human Rights Commission and any other HR monitoring bodies
	
		National Non Governmental Organizations

en.full: Save the Children Strategic Framework for Cooperation 2010 -2012

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
